1 - Senior Media Buyer.mp4

1 . 1 . 1 . 1. all right so let's dive into the senior media buyer outcomes kpis 30 60 90 day ramp okay so The senior media buyer is essentially going to be the person strategizing and coming up with all the media buying strategies. Now, depending on... how your company is structured. You might have them report to a strategist if you have a big enough team for that, or you might be the strategist. as the CEO. So the strategist is the one kind of looking across the whole company and telling, you know, what the whole marketing strategy is. And the senior media buyer is the one actually buying media, looking at the analytics and all that. So, like with every position, we start with the outcomes and the outcomes.

I'll reiterate this again and again: they are really the most important thing because this allows you to fully agree upon what success looks like in this role. So, when I first got started, I really struggled as a manager because I didn't have things like this. It wasn't... you know, there was an objective way of me telling people like, 'Hey, you're doing a good job.' So this makes it much, much easier when you map all this out. And this should roll up to your departmental KPIs. For us, what we care about is— we project a bonus every single month that we want to make, essentially, for uh the company. So the number one outcome we care about is achieving 95% of the projected quarterly bonus compensation. Targets for their assigned clients.

Okay, so this is the number one thing. Now, this might be different for you. It might be the— forecasted. Amount of front end units. It might be the forecasted ROAS. It might not even be a forecast. It could just be a target that you're setting for them. But you need to really understand, like, what is this—actually, what is the number one outcome? And then, for us, we comp the bonus based upon that. So it's pretty simple right here. Number two is achieve 85% of all projected clients. quarterly profit and ROAS goals. So essentially, you know, if you have seven clients for the quarter, month over month, you have 21 projections, 21 different... projections times two metrics equals 42 total. You achieved or exceeded 32 of these projections, which gives you have a 76.

19 achievement. The next thing is it's important to give people a description of what that actually looks like. What you know, there's a lot of times where people will say, 'Maybe I just truly don't understand what that looks like.' You need to give them an example, and you need to give them a full-on measurement, and you need to tell them when is it expected for them to actually report upon this to you. Okay? Number three. Um, if they're a senior, we want them to ramp up people underneath them. So successfully ramp 100% of new junior media buyers to full productivity within three months. You know, essentially ramp up time, KPI achievement, and then 90% of all time tracked in Hubstaff for direct reports and personally. Then we have KPIs.

So what are the actual submetrics for this role? So you're going to have submetrics that you are tracking for them as well. So what is the monthly client churn rate? What is the number of referrals? So for us, it's under 8% monthly. We want at least one referral every single month. Creative win rate. About 25%. Media plan adherence rate, about 90%. Then we have 30, 60, 90-day ramp targets. So days one through 30, we're essentially learning the systems and the processes. We're shadowing the client calls, understanding the forecasting methodology, mastering tracking tools. Days 31 through 60, we're taking over client calls, beginning forecasting bonus, managing creative process. Days 61 through 90, we're hitting the full bonus targets. Maintaining target margins, scaling successful funnels, and then we have strategic growth planning.

Okay. So we have all of this mapped out in a macro level view. So everybody is on the same page and there's no ambiguity around what good looks like. So this makes it very, very easy to ramp people up and hold them accountable. Now, clear performance thresholds that will trigger review or termination: you know, missing more than 50% of leading indicators by day 30. Missing three or more client ROAS targets in a month, two consecutive months below 80% of the forecasted bonus, failure to provide accurate weekly reporting for two plus weeks. Thank you. So you'll get this doc. You'll, if it just depends on, you might have to change it up a little bit. If you're just a brand, you might have to just change up the numbers and make it more brand focus.

If you're running an agency, then obviously some of the stuff will work. Just depends on however your pod is structured. All right, so let's dive into the actual job description now. So like I was saying, the rule for reporting structure. For us, we have a growth marketing manager that this role reports into. Um... This is pretty much the strategist of the pod. If you are a smaller company, this is going to be the CEO. They're going to report into you. You will essentially be sitting in that strategist's seat. And what you want to do with the job description is— you want to pre-sell them. On the role, you know, do you get— do you geek out on media buying and love driving revenue through direct response marketing?

Want to get in the door at— whatever your company is. So for us, it's an extremely fast-growing marketing agency. I want to work with world-class copywriters, videographers, operations, and other media buyers to help clients grow to new heights. Well, then, look no further. So then you're going to sell a little bit about you guys. You know we spend three million dollars a month across these platforms and you'll be helping to to increase that to $5 million a month this year. Some of the clients that we work with are Russell Brunson, Onyx, and Gall, Mike Tyson, Rossofino, and many more. You'll work with a pod of videographers, copywriters, strategists, and data scientists. To plan each kind, forecast the targets we need to hit, blah, blah. Then we go into responsibilities.

So, whatever they're going to be— media buying on. So, for us, it's YouTube, Facebook, TikTok, Google search and display. You know, you're managing upwards of $50 ,000 to $75 ,000 a day on these platforms. You know, some of our clients scale very hard. You're going to be managing forecasting. creating different scenarios that clients targets. You have data-driven decision-making. funnel strategy, creative strategy. um competitor research uh all that then you're gonna have results so these are the results that we went over these are the actual outcomes that we had in the first uh doc okay so these are the exact Results that they are expected to deliver every week every month quarter and year, you know achieving that 95% of projected quarterly bonus on 85% of all projected clients' Quarterly profit and ROAS goals, successfully ramp 100% of new junior media buyers.

Then you have the requirements for them, one plus year's direct response marketing experience. Now, we don't do this in every single rule, but you can. Success in your first two to four months looks like, you know, go over what it actually looks like for them to be successful. Here. Communication expectations. So, hey, you know, how do we actually communicate? We use Slack for internal and client communications. We value over communication. We value speed and communication. You know, we have weekly full team meetings, daily stand-ups with pod leads. And then we get into. You know, we sell them on our company. You know, hey, we were the 207th fastest-growing company on the Yik 5000. We grew, you know, 2,574% over the past three years. Our core values, and then essentially what we offer.

So for the compensation structure, it just really depends on what. you know, budget you have. For this role, if you are tight on budget, you know, if you are a million to two million dollars a year, I would probably go international, and I would be anywhere from 40 to 50 with an offer that allows them to make you know, 70 to 80. If you have a little bit more money, maybe you're a $5 to $10 million company, you could probably hire a senior media buyer that's in the States. Where you could pay them, you know, 75 to 85. My one thing is the better operational structure you have, the more training you have, the better leaders you have. The... easier it is to go out and find talent.

That is in different countries that's a little bit cheaper and train them up. They don't have to be as good. Uh, in, in the States. So like when I started my agency, every, everybody was in house and, you know, essentially I. I started out of a little office that I rented. I eventually bought an office, and then I had everybody in my office, and I trained them up. One by one. And then COVID hit, and we went to more of a remote company. And over the years, we've gotten much better with our operations. We're able to track analytics better. We have a much better operational structure where we're able to have a more remote company. So if you have a... more mature company with operational leaders who really are tracking the day-to-day KPIs and know where everybody needs to be, you could probably go and get less talented people because you could hold them more accountable. If you are a smaller company, you probably want to... If possible, if your budget allows it, get some people that are in-house that you can train up and work with them every single day.
